CREATE TABLE numbers (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO numbers (id, value) VALUES (1, 10), (2, NULL), (3, 20), (4, 30);
CREATE TABLE more_numbers (id INT, value INT);
INSERT INTO more_numbers (id, value) VALUES (1, 5), (2, 15), (3, NULL), (4, 25);
CREATE TABLE combined_numbers AS SELECT id, value FROM numbers UNION SELECT id, value FROM more_numbers;
SELECT sum(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM numbers WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_numbers;
SELECT sum((SELECT value FROM more_numbers WHERE id = 1)) AS total_sum;
SELECT n.id, sum(n.value) FROM numbers n JOIN more_numbers m ON n.id = m.id GROUP BY n.id;
CREATE TABLE complex_numbers (id INT, value INT, category VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO complex_numbers (id, value, category) VALUES (1, 10, 'A'), (2, 20, 'B'), (3, 25, 'A'), (4, NULL, 'B');
SELECT category, sum(value) FROM complex_numbers GROUP BY category;
SELECT sum(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM complex_numbers WHERE category = 'A') AS subquery_a;
SELECT sum(case when value IS NULL then 0 else value end) FROM numbers;
SELECT id, sum(value) AS total FROM (SELECT id, value FROM numbers WHERE value > 0) AS filtered GROUP BY id;
